examples: S CARLIN SPRINGS RD NB @ COLUMBIA PIKE FS -> S Carlin Springs Rd Nb @ Columbia Pike Fs
S ARLINGTON MILL DR EB @ S TAYLOR ST NS -> S Arlington Mill Dr Eb @ S Taylor St Ns
S ARLINGTON MILL DR WB @ S TAYLOR ST FS -> S Arlington Mill Dr Wb @ S Taylor St Fs
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 574 of 574 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 2116 of 2116 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 557 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'leading_or_trailing_whitespaces' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Leading or trailing whitespaces (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
findings.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
findings.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 6 kinds of issue across 15366 instances (0 error, 15366 warning, 0 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 335 days.

findings.fix: Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The service alerts realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 1 of 14 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 6 times: 2 of 3 feeds healthy; 92.9% of scheduled trips had live predictions; 100.0% of vehicles on their route; predictions ran a median of 18s behind schedule.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 338 days.
States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Your feed uses agency_id 1; the NTD asks for your five-digit NTD ID (30080) so the feed lines up with your record.
ntd_id_alignment.fix: Change the agency_id to 30080 in agency.txt and update the matching agency_id in routes.txt.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 338 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 573 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("S CARLIN SPRINGS RD NB @ COLUMBIA PIKE FS", "S ARLINGTON MILL DR EB @ S TAYLOR ST NS", "S ARLINGTON MILL DR WB @ S TAYLOR ST FS", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

findings.effort: A cleanup pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove stops no route serves, or add the trips that should call at them.
findings.what: 1 of 574 boardable stops are never served by any trip.
findings.why: Riders see these stops in apps and on the map but can never catch anything there, which erodes trust in the data.

top_fixes.fix: Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
top_fixes.what: The service alerts realtime feed failed during sampling.
top_fixes.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

top_fixes.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 574 of 574 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

top_fixes.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
top_fixes.what: 2116 of 2116 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
top_fixes.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.
